clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) and leukocytosis (14.2 K/uL) meet Sepsis-3 criteria for sepsis risk. Thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL) with SOFA coagulation score ≥1, anemia (Hgb 9.8 g/dL), and bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) suggest hepatic involvement. AKI stage 3 (creatinine 8.9 mg/dL) was flagged but creatinine 8.9 mg/dL was excluded as a rapid delta outlier (6.5 mg/dL rise in 6 hours).

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and creatinine elevation meet Sepsis-3 criteria (suspected infection + SOFA increase ≥2)
Creatinine elevation meets KDIGO criteria for AKI
prioritized_risks.threshold: Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or ≥1.5× baseline

prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coag score ≥1
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but hypotension/vasopressor requirement not documented
prioritized_risks.threshold: Vasopressor-dependent hypotension + lactate >2 mmol/L after fluids

recommended_actions: Initiate sepsis protocol per Sepsis-3 guidelines
Order blood cultures and broad-spectrum antibiotics
Monitor AKI progression with hourly urine output and creatinine trends
Consider platelet transfusion if platelets <50 K/uL or active bleeding
doctor_handoff: Review for hypotension requiring vasopressors to confirm septic shock. Confirm AKI stage with corrected creatinine values. Assess for coagulopathy progression.

disease_progression.observation: Baseline abnormalities: WBC 14.2 K/uL, Hgb 9.8 g/dL, Bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL
AKI stage 3 (Cr 8.9 mg/dL excluded as outlier), platelets 88 K/uL

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
